message:
feat(core): exponential histogram for latency/size distribution tracking (wa-y4i3)

Add `exp_histogram` module implementing a log-scale bucket histogram for
efficient, bounded-memory distribution tracking with O(1) record and
O(buckets) percentile computation.

ExpHistogram:
- Configurable base (default 2.0) and bucket count (default 32) giving
  power-of-two boundaries: [0, base), [base, base²), [base², base³), ...
- `record(value)`: O(1) insertion via log-base bucket index computation
- `percentile(p)`: linear scan through cumulative counts to find the
  bucket containing the p-th percentile, returning the bucket midpoint
  as the estimate
- `count()`, `min()`, `max()`, `mean()` summary statistics
- `merge(&other)`: combines two histograms with matching configurations
  for distributed aggregation
- `reset()`: clears all buckets for epoch-based windowing
- Serde Serialize/Deserialize for snapshot persistence

Intended use cases in FrankenTerm:
- Per-pane capture latency distribution (p50/p90/p99 reporting)
- IPC message size distribution for capacity planning
- Agent process lifetime tracking
- Actual vs configured polling interval distributions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
